title: BabelQueueMessageEnvelope
description: The canonical, language-agnostic BabelQueue wire envelope (schema_version 1). This schema is authoritative alongside contracts/message-envelope.md.

job.description: The message URN — language-agnostic identity. Canonical producer field name. Consumers also accept 'urn' as an inbound alias. Never a class name.

urn.description: Inbound alias for 'job'. Accepted by consumers for interoperability; producers SHOULD emit 'job'.

trace_id.description: Cross-service correlation id. Generated by the first producer, preserved and forwarded unchanged by every SDK across every hop.

data.description: Pure, JSON-encodable business payload. No language-specific types. Numbers/time/binary per contracts/message-envelope.md section 6.

id.description: Unique id for THIS message (distinct from trace_id).

queue.description: Logical queue name (not the broker key).

schema_version.description: Envelope schema version. Consumers MUST reject versions they do not support.

created_at.description: Production time as Unix epoch MILLISECONDS, UTC.

attempts.description: Top-level transport retry counter, mutated by the broker/worker. Deliberately kept OUT of the immutable meta block.

dead_letter.description: Optional. Present ONLY on messages sitting on a dead-letter queue (ADR-0009). Additive, so it does not change schema_version. Normal consumers ignore it.

exception.description: Producer-language exception/type name, if any (informational, language-specific).

failed_at.description: Dead-letter time as Unix epoch milliseconds, UTC.

original_queue.description: The queue the message was consumed from before dead-lettering.

lang.description: Language of the SDK that dead-lettered the message.
